frameworks.name: recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: trained RNNs as model substrate for perceptual decision-making circuits

frameworks.name: dynamical systems analysis
frameworks.one_line: network solutions interpreted via dynamical structure underlying decisions

frameworks.name: causal circuit perturbation
frameworks.one_line: framework for interpreting artificial inactivation experiments in neural circuits

core_question: How can the dynamical structure of recurrent neural circuits explain the behavioral consequences of partial inactivation?
key_assumptions: perceptual decision behavior emerges from recurrent dynamics
trained RNNs are an adequate proxy for biological decision circuits
dynamical system structure governs sensitivity to perturbation
manipulations: partial inactivation of units in trained recurrent networks
perceptual decision task as behavioral readout
key_findings: dynamical structure predicts magnitude of behavioral effects from perturbations
complex circuits compensate and adapt to partial inactivation
certain behavioral features are most sensitive to perturbation
interpretations: dynamical-systems lens provides a precise account of perturbation effects on behavior
design strategies can improve interpretability of inactivation experiments
